title: TCP Connections, Accept Loops, Half-Closes, Deadlines, and Failure · Contract Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded TCP connection lifecycle state machine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain ordered reliable byte-stream delivery while accounting for segmentation, retransmission, flow control, congestion, and missing message boundaries.
Assign listener, accepted connection, goroutine, buffer, deadline, and close ownership with bounded accept-error handling.
Set and refresh absolute read and write deadlines by protocol phase and classify timeout evidence without assuming cancellation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Write Ambiguity Retry
steps.instruction:
Order the Write Ambiguity Retry evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Write Ambiguity Retry.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Write Ambiguity Retry assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Write Ambiguity Retry, keep Write Ambiguity Retry connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug protocol team handles case 7689 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify connection, deadline, completion, and close state without inventing message boundaries; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat failed or timed-out writes as potentially partially observed and avoid blind replay of non-idempotent protocol effects.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Write Ambiguity Retry has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat failed or timed-out writes as potentially partially observed and avoid blind replay of non-idempotent protocol effects.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Write Ambiguity Retry, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Write Ambiguity Retry precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Write Ambiguity Retry, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 7697 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify connection, deadline, completion, and close state without inventing message boundaries; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat failed or timed-out writes as potentially partially observed and avoid blind replay of non-idempotent protocol effects.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Write Ambiguity Retry, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A failed socket write proves the peer received none of the bytes.
